ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "screen_name" TEXT /* Screen name of the user on the social media platform */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user's account (hashed for security) */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Short bio of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "tweet_content" TEXT /* Content of the tweet */,
  "tweet_date" TEXT /* Date when the tweet was posted */,
  "number_of_likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "number_of_retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet has received */,
  "number_of_replies" INTEGER /* Number of replies the tweet has received */,
  "is_reply" INTEGER /* Whether the tweet is a reply to another tweet */,
  "parent_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent tweet (if the tweet is a reply) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_parent_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the topic */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the topic */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the topic (e.g., technology, sports, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_topics" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic discussed by the user */,
  "last_discussed_date" TEXT /* Date when the user last discussed the topic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "total_tweets" INTEGER /* Total number of tweets posted by the user */,
  "total_topics" INTEGER /* Total number of topics discussed by the user */,
  "total_likes" INTEGER /* Total number of likes received by the user */,
  "total_retweets" INTEGER /* Total number of retweets received by the user */,
  "average_likes_per_tweet" REAL /* Average number of likes per tweet */,
  "average_retweets_per_tweet" REAL /* Average number of retweets per tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user for whom the report is generated */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_management" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was assigned to the user */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was revoked from the user (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logins" (
  "login_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each login record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "login_date" TEXT /* Date when the user logged in */,
  "login_time" TEXT /* Time when the user logged in */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("login_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logins_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_resets" (
  "reset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password reset record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "reset_date" TEXT /* Date when the password reset was requested */,
  "reset_time" TEXT /* Time when the password reset was requested */,
  "reset_token" TEXT /* Token sent to the user for password reset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_resets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
